title: Maps (Dictionary) 🗂️
description: Store items using Key-Value pairs for quick lookups.

slides.title: The Dictionary 📖
slides.content:
# Key-Value Pairs

If you want to look up a word in a dictionary, you don't read page 1, then page 2, searching for it... you just look up the EXACT word (Key) to find the definition (Value) instantly.

In Dart, a **Map** associates a unique **Key** with a specific **Value**.

slides.title: Creating a Map 🗺️

quizData.question: What separates the Key from its Value inside a Map?
quizData.explanation: A colon ':' pairs the Key on the left to the Value on the right.

slides.title: Accessing Data 🔍
slides.content:
## No Indexes needed!

To retrieve data from a List, we used an index number: `list[0]`.
To retrieve data from a Map, we use the EXACT KEY!

`int topScore = playerScores['Player3']; // Retrieves 9999`

If you ask for a key that doesn't exist (`playerScores['Bob']`), Dart automatically returns `null` (nothing).

quizData.question:
If you have a Map named users = {'admin': 'John'}; 
How do you retrieve 'John'? print( users[_] );
quizData.explanation: Pass the exact key (as a string) in the square brackets: users['admin']

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Watch how we can add new Keys on the fly!
